source_metadata.title_raw: Landon Sudholt - A-25-069 - May 7, 2025 -  San Andreas

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3050 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 7, 2025


Landon Sudholt
Recorder Clerk I
Calaveras County Elections Office
891 Mountain Ranch Road
San Andreas, CA 95249
Via email at: electionsweb@calaverascounty.gov

Re:	Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-25-069

Dear Landon Sudholt:

	This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of the County of Calaveras
regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

	May the committees involved in the County's August 26, 2025, election combine the semi-
annual statement with the first pre-election statement due July 17, 2025?

CONCLUSION

	In connection with the August 26, 2025, election, committees may use a filing schedule that
combines the semi-annual campaign statement with the first pre-election statement. The combined
statement will be due on July 17, 2025, covering the period January 1, 2025, through July 12, 2025.

ANALYSIS

	Committees are required to file two pre-election campaign statements during an election,
disclosing contributions received and expenditures made in connection with that election. These
statements are filed 40 days before the election and again 12 days before the election. (Section
84200.) Semi-annual statements are filed on January 31 and July 31, respectively. (Section
84200.)

	The County of Calaveras is holding a special election on August 26, 2025. The first semi-
annual report for 2025, covering the period January 1 through June 30, is due on July 31, 2025. The
first pre-election statement filed in connection with this election would then cover the period of July

_______________________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18104 through 18998 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-25-069
Page No. 2

1 through July 12. It would be due on July 17, 2025, before the deadline for the semi-annual
statement. The second pre-election statement is due on August 14, 2025, and covers the reporting
period of July 13 through August 9.

There is a mechanism in the Act that permits combining statements when an overlapping
reporting period occurs, or the filing of the semi-annual statement would be an additional,
unnecessary filing to the two pre-election statements. (Section 84205.) Combining the first pre-
election statement with the semi-annual statement would be appropriate for the County's August
26, 2025, election.

Accordingly, specified committees involved with the August 26, 2025 measure appearing on
the ballot may combine the semi-annual statement with the first pre-election statement. The
statement will cover the period of January 1 through July 12 and be due on July 17, 2025. A
campaign statement filing schedule that outlines the due dates for filing statements is enclosed for
your convenience.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Dave Bainbridge
General Counsel

By: Erika M. Boyd
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

EMB:aja

sections.question:
May the committees involved in the County’s August 26, 2025, election combine the semi-
annual statement with the first pre-election statement due July 17, 2025?
sections.conclusion:
In connection with the August 26, 2025, election, committees may use a filing schedule that 
combines the semi-annual campaign statement with the first pre-election statement. The combined 
statement will be due on July 17, 2025, covering the period January 1, 2025, through July 12, 2025.
sections.analysis:
Committees are required to file two pre-election campaign statements during an election, 
disclosing contributions received and expenditures made in connection with that election. These 
statements are filed 40 days before the election and again 12 days before the election. (Section 
84200.8.) Semi-annual statements are filed on January 31 and July 31, respectively. (Section 
84200.)
The County of Calaveras is holding a special election on August 26, 2025. The first semi-
annual report for 2025, covering the period January 1 through June 30, is due on July 31, 2025. The 
first pre-election statement filed in connection with this election would then cover the period of July1 through July 12. It would be due on July 17, 2025, before the deadline for the semi-annual 
statement. The second pre-election statement is due on August 14, 2025, and covers the reporting 
period of July 13 through August 9.
There is a mechanism in the Act that permits combining statements when an overlapping 
reporting period occurs, or the filing of the semi-annual statement would be an additional, 
unnecessary filing to the two pre-election statements. (Section 84205.) Combining the first pre-
election statement with the semi-annual statement would be appropriate for the County’s August 
26, 2025, election. 
Accordingly, specified committees involved with the August 26, 2025 measure appearing on 
the ballot may combine the semi-annual statement with the first pre-election statement. The 
statement will cover the period of January 1 through July 12 and be due on July 17, 2025. A 
campaign statement filing schedule that outlines the due dates for filing statements is enclosed for 
your convenience.
